dev.jeka.core.api.tooling.docker

Class JkDockerBuild

  • java.lang.Object
    • dev.jeka.core.api.tooling.docker.JkDockerBuild
  • Direct Known Subclasses:
    JkDockerJvmBuild, JkDockerNativeBuild

    public class JkDockerBuild
extends java.lang.Object
    Docker Builder assistant for defining and creating Docker images.

    This class facilitates the programmatic construction of a Docker build context directory and the execution of the docker build command using that context.

    Key Features

    • Configuring the base image
    • Adding a non-root user to the image
    • Customizing the build process with additional steps in the generated Dockerfile

    Docker Image Build Process

    1. A static template of the Dockerfile is generated. This template may include tokens such as ${baseImage}, allowing flexibility when the base image or user creation steps need to be determined dynamically.
    2. Tokens in the static template are resolved dynamically to produce the final Dockerfile.
    3. The build context directory is created. The generated Dockerfile and any specified files are copied into this directory.
    4. The docker build command is executed using the generated build context directory. This operation requires Docker CLI (e.g., Docker Desktop) installed on the host machine.

    Default Dockerfile Template

    The default Dockerfile template is as follows:

    
 FROM ${baseImage}
 ${addNonRootUser}

    Users can customize this template by adding steps to the dockerFileTemplate field. Custom tokens (e.g., ${myToken}) can also be defined, and their resolution logic specified using the #addTokenResolver(String, Supplier<String>) method.

    Importing Files

    Files can be imported into the build context using:

    • #addFsOperation()
    • dockerFileTemplate#addCopy(fileSystemPath, containerPath)

    Building and Pushing Images

    Once the Docker build is configured, the image can be generated using the buildImage(String imageName) method. The resulting image will be automatically pushed to the Docker registry.

    Intermediate Steps

    Intermediate steps such as dockerFileTemplate.render(), renderDockerFile(), or generateContextDir() can be used to aid in the configuration of the Docker build process.

    Extensibility

    This class can be extended to provide a specific default template and additional convenience methods.

      JkDockerBuild addFsOperation(java.util.function.Consumer<java.nio.file.Path> fsOperation)
      Adds an operation to be executed immediately after the build context directory is created.
      JkDockerBuild addTokenResolver(java.lang.String token, java.util.function.Supplier<java.lang.Object> valueSupplier)
      Specifies how to resolve the given token.
      void buildImage(java.nio.file.Path buildContextDir, java.lang.String imageName)
      Builds the Docker image with the specified image name.

      Similar to buildImage(Path, String), but without requiring a context directory to be specified.

      Generates the build context directory without building the Docker image.

      java.util.List<java.lang.Integer> getExposedPorts()
      Returns an unmodifiable list of exposed ports in the Docker image.

      Returns a string representation of the command line arguments for port mapping in the Docker image.

      Checks whether the image includes a step to create a non-root user.

      JkDockerBuild setAddUserStatement(java.lang.String addUserTemplate)
      Sets the Docker statement for adding a 'nonroot' user.
      The placeholders "${UID}" and "${GID}" will be replaced with the user ID and group ID configured for this instance, respectively.

      Adds a port to be exposed by the container.

      Sets the group ID for the 'nonroot' user.

      Sets the user ID for the 'nonroot' user.

      Transform a list as [a,b,c] in a string as "a", "b", "c"

    • Method Detail

      • of

        public static JkDockerBuild of()
        Creates a instannce.

      • setBaseImage

        public final JkDockerBuild setBaseImage(java.lang.String baseImage)
        Sets the base image name to use a s the base image in the docker file.

      • getBaseImage

        public java.lang.String getBaseImage()

      • setNonRootUserCreationMode

        public final JkDockerBuild setNonRootUserCreationMode(JkDockerBuild.NonRootMode nonRootUserCreationMode)

      • hasNonRootUserCreation

        public final boolean hasNonRootUserCreation()
        Checks whether the image includes a step to create a non-root user.
        Returns:
        true if a non-root user creation step is present, false otherwise.

      • setAddUserStatement

        public final JkDockerBuild setAddUserStatement(java.lang.String addUserTemplate)
        Sets the Docker statement for adding a 'nonroot' user.
        The placeholders "${UID}" and "${GID}" will be replaced with the user ID and group ID configured for this instance, respectively.

      • setAddUserStatement

        public final JkDockerBuild setAddUserStatement(JkDockerBuild.AddUserStatement addUserTemplate)
        See Also:
        setAddUserStatement(String)

      • setUserId

        public final JkDockerBuild setUserId(int userId)
        Sets the user ID for the 'nonroot' user.
        Parameters:
        userId - the user ID to assign.

      • setGroupId

        public final JkDockerBuild setGroupId(int groupId)
        Sets the group ID for the 'nonroot' user.
        Parameters:
        groupId - the group ID to assign.

      • setExposedPorts

        public final JkDockerBuild setExposedPorts(java.lang.Integer... ports)
        Adds a port to be exposed by the container.

      • getExposedPorts

        public final java.util.List<java.lang.Integer> getExposedPorts()
        Returns an unmodifiable list of exposed ports in the Docker image.

      • getPortMappingArgs

        protected final java.lang.String getPortMappingArgs()
        Returns a string representation of the command line arguments for port mapping in the Docker image.

      • addTokenResolver

        public JkDockerBuild addTokenResolver(java.lang.String token,
                                      java.util.function.Supplier<java.lang.Object> valueSupplier)
        Specifies how to resolve the given token. The value supplier can return any type of object, which will be converted to a String using the Object.toString() method.

      • addFsOperation

        public JkDockerBuild addFsOperation(java.util.function.Consumer<java.nio.file.Path> fsOperation)
        Adds an operation to be executed immediately after the build context directory is created.

        This is typically used to import files or directories into the context directory.

        Parameters:
        fsOperation - a Consumer that accepts the path to the build context directory.

      • renderDockerfile

        public java.lang.String renderDockerfile()
        Render the final Dockerfile.

      • buildImage

        public void buildImage(java.nio.file.Path buildContextDir,
                       java.lang.String imageName)
        Builds the Docker image with the specified image name.
        Parameters:
        buildContextDir - the directory where the Dockerfile will be generated and from which COPY paths will be resolved.
        imageName - the name of the image to build. It may include a tag name.

      • buildImageInTemp

        public java.nio.file.Path buildImageInTemp(java.lang.String imageName)
        Similar to buildImage(Path, String), but without requiring a context directory to be specified.

        A temporary context directory will be created in a random location.

        Returns:
        the path of the created context directory.

      • generateContextDir

        public void generateContextDir(java.nio.file.Path buildContextDir)
        Generates the build context directory without building the Docker image. This can be useful for debugging purposes.

        This method is automatically called when buildImage(Path, String) is invoked.

      • renderInfo

        public java.lang.String renderInfo()
        Renders a human-readable string representation of the information used to build the image.

      • computeImportedFileRelativePath

        protected java.lang.String computeImportedFileRelativePath(java.nio.file.Path fileToCopy,
                                                           java.lang.String targetDirRelPath)

      • toDoubleQuotedArgs

        protected static java.lang.String toDoubleQuotedArgs(java.lang.String... args)
        Transform a list as [a,b,c] in a string as "a", "b", "c"
